steps.title: Spot the plural reference
steps.content: “Two jugglers” and “for both” signal that the blank must use plural forms.

steps.title: Apply agreement in number
steps.content:
“Jugglers, regardless of their” satisfies agreement in number: “jugglers” is plural, matching the subjects, and “their” is the plural possessive.

The others either violate plurality or use the wrong homophone—”they're” (contraction) and “there” (adverb) instead of possessive “their”.

That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
